response_text:
# Reliable Sources for Government AI Governance Frameworks

## Primary/Official Government Sources

**United States**
- White House Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP)
- NIST AI Risk Management Framework (ai.gov, nist.gov)
- Congressional Research Service reports
- Federal Register notices and executive orders

**European Union**
- EUR-Lex (official EU law database)
- European Commission official publications
- European Parliament legislative documents

**International Bodies**
- OECD AI Policy Observatory (oecd.ai)
- UNESCO AI Ethics recommendations
- ITU AI for Good publications
- G7/G20 official communiqués

## Academic & Research Institutions

- Brookings Institution AI governance research
- Georgetown CSET (Center for Security and Emerging Technology)
- Oxford Internet Institute
- MIT Technology Policy program
- Stanford HAI (Human-Centered AI)

## Quality Indicators to Check

- [ ] Primary source documents over secondary summaries
- [ ] Publication dates (this field changes rapidly)
- [ ] Author credentials and institutional affiliation
- [ ] Peer review status where applicable
- [ ] Whether analysis distinguishes proposals from enacted policy

## Practical Cautions

- **Verify currency** - frameworks update frequently
- **Check jurisdiction** - frameworks vary significantly by country
- **Distinguish binding vs. voluntary** guidelines
- **Cross-reference** multiple sources for contested interpretations

Would you like recommendations for a specific jurisdiction or policy area?

## Primary/Official Government Sources

**United States**
- White House Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP)
- NIST AI Risk Management Framework (ai.gov, nist.gov)
- Congressional Research Service reports
- Federal Register notices and executive orders

**European Union**
- EUR-Lex (official EU law database)
- European Commission official publications
- European Parliament legislative documents

**International Bodies**
- OECD AI Policy Observatory (oecd.ai)
- UNESCO AI Ethics recommendations
- ITU AI for Good publications
- G7/G20 official communiqués

## Academic & Research Institutions

- Brookings Institution AI governance research
- Georgetown CSET (Center for Security and Emerging Technology)
- Oxford Internet Institute
- MIT Technology Policy program
- Stanford HAI (Human-Centered AI)

## Quality Indicators to Check

- [ ] Primary source documents over secondary summaries
- [ ] Publication dates (this field changes rapidly)
- [ ] Author credentials and institutional affiliation
- [ ] Peer review status where applicable
- [ ] Whether analysis distinguishes proposals from enacted policy

## Practical Cautions

- **Verify currency** - frameworks update frequently
- **Check jurisdiction** - frameworks vary significantly by country
